audit_items.proof_trace: Multiple unchecked malloc() returns are immediately dereferenced in _add_run (L129-130: run->visual_next after malloc, L135-137: run->chars/positions after second malloc) and ucstring_create (L326-331: str->cursor_motion after malloc), with no NULL guards. Separately, ucstring_cursor_info (L735-737) dereferences str->cursor without a NULL check: ucstring_create returns a valid ucstring_t with cursor==0 when start==end (early return at L342 skips the L430 cursor initialisation), so any subsequent cursor call on that empty-string object NPDs.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker sending LDAP requests that cause the bidirectional text library to process an empty string (start==end) and then invoke a cursor function (ucstring_cursor_right/left/info) will trigger a NULL dereference crash in the OpenLDAP process, achieving denial of service; allocation-failure paths in _add_run and ucstring_create provide additional NPD vectors under memory pressure.
